steps.title: Compare the dates
steps.content: Grande Terre completely emerged 37 million years ago. Durand et al. found that the southern beech clade on Grande Terre has a crown age of 16.4 million years, much more recent than the island's emergence.

steps.title: Reject unsupported extensions
steps.content: Choice A makes an unsupported claim about origin outside the South Pacific. Choice B contradicts the 16.4-million-year age. Choice D makes a claim about most species on Grande Terre, but the passage only discusses southern beeches.

steps.title: Draw the age inference
steps.content: A clade with a most recent common ancestor 16.4 million years ago did not exist before the island emerged 37 million years ago. The answer is C.
